event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins on a city street with a man in a teal tie-dye hoodie and a man in a black hoodie playfully bantering while holding bananas, with the former exclaiming 'Jam!' and the latter questioning, 'Why are you trying to eat my bread, fam?' The scene shifts to a close-up of ketchup being squeezed onto a plate, followed by another man in a black hoodie at an outdoor café lamenting, 'I feel so fucking sick' and 'This is grim' while eating pizza, prompting a reaction of 'My God.' The setting then transitions to a large, covered public space in London, identified by 'Nespresso' and 'Hippodrome Casino' signage and a red double-decker bus, where a group of five casually dressed men, including the pizza eater, are gathered for what appears to be a filming session, with a man in a black tracksuit with beanie operating a camera on a stabilizer. The pizza eater, now the main speaker, announces, 'Welcome to another Sidemen race,' detailing the challenge of 'eating a different food for every letter in the alphabet' within 'three and a half hours or so,' with rules including 'splitting into two teams,' 'can't go to the same place twice,' and 'everyone on the team has to eat the entirety of the item.' The group, including a man in a light blue hoodie checking his phone, interacts with gestures and laughter, concluding with a surprised 'Oh, fuck!' The scene then shifts to a bright, modern public plaza under a large white dome, possibly a theme park, where the group of five men, wearing coordinated streetwear with green circular logos, are engaged in a playful interaction. The Man in black tracksuit with beanie continues filming. The discussion turns to challenge rules, with one explaining that finishing a large dish is mandatory, eliciting a 'Jesus' reaction. Strategizing ensues, with a note of 'No wasted.' As a Ferris wheel is revealed, a voice asks, 'What are the teams, Zek?' Zek announces the first team: 'Simon, Toby, Harry, and JJ,' met with cheers. The other team is declared, leading to humorous exchanges about eating capabilities, such as 'I've got tons that don't eat, bro!' and 'Look at the size of him!' The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie comments, 'Of course he's not finishing the fucking meal!' followed by laughter and an admission of 'I'm actually cutting cheap, you know.' The group attempts to decide on a team name, with 'The Blacks and the Whites' suggested, met with playful reactions like 'Sure, chess, chess, chess!' before ending with uncertainty about their team identity.

event_blocks.summary: In a bright, modern open-air plaza, a group of five men engaged in animated conversation, with one encouraging "have faith" and another stating, "We're team." The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, holding a banana, prompted playful banter like "Reverse Oreo." The Man in light blue hoodie checked his phone, while a voice questioned, "Why are you fucking catting me?" The Man in black tracksuit with beanie filmed them. The group discussed a "bakery" and "apples" before dispersing, with the Man in light blue hoodie walking away first, followed by the others, as a voice bid, "good luck guys." The camera followed them past a 'Simit Sarayi' kiosk and a 'FAST TRACK' sign into an urban setting. Later, the Man in black hoodie (first) ran toward the camera, joined by the Man in white hoodie and the Man in light blue hoodie near a red Lime JUMP bike. The Man in black hoodie (second) and the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie also joined, with playful banter about being a "good team." The Man in light blue hoodie sat on the bike, responding, "I'm not like reckless, it's fine" when told to "sit on the seat." The Man in black tracksuit with beanie continued filming. The Man in white hoodie pointed off-screen as the Man in light blue hoodie rode the bike away, prompting exclamations of "Oh my god!" The camera panned to a fruit stand with apples and oranges, as the group discussed "apple," "turnover," and "pastry." A close-up of a hand holding a banana followed, with talk of a "supermarket thing." On a bustling city sidewalk, the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie gestured while speaking to the Man in black hoodie (first) near a fruit stand. A red double-decker bus passed as they debated an apple-eating challenge, with one voice frustrated, "these videos are fucking shite" and "You have to eat an apple and this day's ruined." They noted the fruit stand lacked apples, prompting a voice to repeatedly state, "Oh, man, I can't eat banana, man." The Man in black hoodie (second) and the Man in white hoodie walked across the street, followed by the Man in light blue hoodie entering a fast-food restaurant. Exiting, they walked past a red-facade Walgreens store. The Man in black tracksuit with beanie continued filming. The group walked toward a Walgreens convenience store, with the Man in black tracksuit with beanie filming. Inside, shelves of snacks and drinks were visible. Exiting, the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie held a red apple, and the Man in black hoodie (first) held a phone. Comments about a "race" where "they won" were made, and a voice requested, "Guys, I need a knife." At a street corner, a male voice complained, "My apple's a bit dented," while another praised, "This is a good apple," leading to a pun, "Apple pay." The Man in light blue hoodie was seen riding a red Lime JUMP bike. The video ended with the group walking past a fast-food restaurant and a fruit stand, with a final instruction, "You gotta twist it, remember?" followed by an exclamation of "Oh!" and a distinct twisting sound. The Man in black hoodie (first) initiated a conversation about 'strength' and bananas, while the Man in white hoodie gestured animatedly. The bearded Man in a teal tie-dye hoodie, holding an apple, expressed a strong dislike for bananas, stating, 'It makes me like throw up.' The conversation shifted to food, with voices discussing a bakery and the Man in black hoodie (first) suggesting, 'Let's go.' Passing a 'Walgreens' store and a Coca-Cola vending machine, the focus turned to their red apples, with comments on how 'shiny it goes' and 'the state of the apple.' The Man in black hoodie (first) declared, 'I got a Josh apple,' followed by laughter. The discussion continued about food, with a voice mentioning a burger, aligning with their passage by a 'Slug & Lettuce' restaurant. The Man in black tracksuit with a beanie actively filmed, asking, 'You guys enjoying this?' as distinct 'Mmm' sounds of eating apples were heard. The conversation shifted to biscuits, with a voice reminding, 'Remember we have to finish all the biscuits,' and another suggesting, 'Let's get a small pack of biscuits.' The urban setting provided a dynamic backdrop as they passed an 'M&S FOODHALL' entrance.

raw_segments: The video opens with a group of four men walking along a wet urban sidewalk under a London overpass, passing modern buildings and storefronts. The Man in white hoodie, Man in light blue hoodie, Man in black hoodie (second), and Man in black hoodie (first) are engaged in a lively discussion. One voice, identified as the Man in black hoodie (second), asks about getting 'dumplings' from a restaurant, while another suggests getting 'fish in Wasabi,' referring to the 'wasabi' sushi & bento shop they pass, where the Man in black hoodie (second) briefly looks inside. The conversation continues about 'salmon nigiri' and 'mini donuts,' with the Man in black hoodie (first) also contributing. As they walk past a 'Dallas Chicken & Fish' restaurant and a 'South Bank' sign, the Man in black hoodie (second) animatedly gestures while speaking, asking if 'doballs' count as 'bread,' a point confirmed by other voices. He then playfully exclaims that his 'judge,' Cal Freezy, is 'online' and has ruled that 'salmon' does not count as 'fish' but as 'S,' and 'fries' count as 'fries' rather than 'F' or 'P for potato.' The camera then shifts to the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, who walks alone along a different overcast and damp street, adjusting his face mask and smiling. He passes iconic red telephone boxes, a 'Cards Galore' store, and the 'itsu' restaurant, with a red double-decker bus visible in the background, reinforcing the London setting.

The video opens with a group of men walking through a wet, overcast urban environment in London, with one voice commenting on the 'lovely English weather.' The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie adjusts his face mask as they pass a 'Virgin Active' sign. The conversation quickly turns to food, with a voice suggesting, 'Leon, get some fries,' as they move past the 'LEON' restaurant. This sparks an animated debate about 'doballs,' with one voice insisting, 'They don't do doballs, bro,' while another argues, 'They must do doballs, bro, it's an Italian,' likely referencing the 'Azzurro Italian Bar & Kitchen' they pass with its outdoor seating. The Man in white hoodie and the Man in light blue hoodie are seen conversing and checking a menu board, both holding small objects that appear to be snacks, actively participating in the discussion. Multiple voices confirm, 'There's no doballs,' before the group collectively realizes their mistake, excitedly exclaiming, 'Doughnuts! Doughnuts!' A voice then remarks, 'Christ almighty. Well, they do doughnuts?' The Man in black hoodie (first), wearing a 'SOMN' hoodie and holding a snack, walks alongside them. The Man in black hoodie (second), wearing a green circular logo on his hoodie, gestures animatedly and raises his fist in a celebratory gesture, reflecting the group's lively interaction. The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie is seen again, adjusting his mask and smiling, seemingly amused by the conversation. The discussion then shifts to fries, with one voice humorously stating, 'Even if you order small fries in Five Guys, they're gonna fill the fucking bag,' to which another agrees, 'Oh, true.' The group continues their casual exploration, passing a 'Caffè Nero,' with the wet pavement reflecting the overcast sky and urban architecture.

The video opens on a bustling, overcast London street, its wet pavement reflecting city lights and storefronts like Bella Italia, O2, and Topshop, as a group of three men walks along. Leading the way is the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, later identified as Harry, who smiles and occasionally adjusts his face mask. Beside him, the Man in black hoodie (first), wearing a 'SOMN' hoodie and displaying hand tattoos, gestures animatedly while speaking in a strong, opinionated voice, complaining about food choices like focaccia and fig, and emphatically stating, "I want to eat a fucking fry," adding, "It's pissing down with rain and you want me to eat fig?". Slightly behind, the Man in black hoodie (second), with a green circular logo on his hoodie, frequently checks his phone and is heard suggesting, "Let's just get fries, man." As they pass iconic red telephone boxes, a Pizza Hut, and a Halifax bank, with a red double-decker bus in the background, the constant city bustle and traffic sounds fill the air. The group then enters an M&S FOODHALL, where the Man in black hoodie (second) picks up a pink box of 'Yumnuts'. The scene shifts to a McDonald’s, where Harry, the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, stands at a self-service kiosk, inserting a card to place an order. Here, the Man in black hoodie (second) asks Harry about his donut preference, to which Harry replies, requesting a "pink sprinkly one," before the Man in black hoodie (second) announces he's "going to the toilet," as they finalize their order for four donuts.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a McDonald’s self-service kiosk displaying a payment prompt, followed by laughter. The scene shifts to a bus stop on April 26th, where four men, each with a pink McDonald’s snack box, are seated on a red bench. The Man in light blue hoodie eats, while the Man in white hoodie expresses doubt about their progress in a word game, countered by the Man in black hoodie with a green circular logo. Amidst laughter, they discuss being "on E" and guess words like "Egg" and "Eggplant." The Man in black hoodie with 'SOMN' on his chest critiques his donut, though the Man in light blue hoodie finds his acceptable. On June 26th, three men, including the Man in black hoodie with 'SOMN' and the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, are on a wet city sidewalk eating McDonald’s, with the Man in black hoodie with 'SOMN' questioning the small fries and their location. Returning to the bus stop, the four men converse with phones, punctuated by laughter and an exaggerated "Oh my god." Upbeat electronic music plays as they discuss a "pizza place." The scene then shifts to a bus stop with four men under a shelter, eating McDonald’s. A voice mentions heading for "edamame," 0.2 miles away. The Man in light blue hoodie gestures as one exclaims, "That was f***ing good." A playful exchange about fetching grapes ensues. Two other men, the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie and the Man in black hoodie with 'SOMN', walk on a wet city sidewalk eating McDonald’s, with a red double-decker bus and black cabs passing a Sainsbury’s store. They regroup outside a glass-fronted building, joined by the first two men. The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie gives a bottle to the Man in black hoodie with 'SOMN', who holds strawberries. The group then moves to a dimly lit restaurant, eating sushi around a long wooden table, discussing "dumplings" and "grape." One man declares fullness but still wants "a grape." The "edamame has arrived!" is announced, followed by laughter and an exclamation of "Hey boys, no... wait." The video opens inside an 'itsu' restaurant with a group of five men around a wooden table with Tsingtao beer, edamame, and chopsticks, engaged in lively conversation and laughter. The Man in white hoodie toasts with his beer, while the Man in black hoodie (first) with 'SOMN' talks animatedly, and the Man in black hoodie (second) with the green circular logo films. Their discussion touches on finishing food and drinking beer, with repeated exclamations of "chess." The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie is briefly seen on an urban sidewalk eating McDonald’s, with comments about "Ham!" and difficulty incorporating it. Back in the restaurant, laughter erupts over how Harry holds a glass, with challenges and disbelief expressed. The conversation continues about dropping glasses and using thumbs, with the statement, "It just doesn't feel natural at all." The scene shifts to an overcast, wet urban sidewalk near a black telephone booth with a 'WiFi here' sign, where the Man in black hoodie (first) and the Man in black hoodie (second) stand, with a red double-decker bus passing. They discuss food, with a voice asking, "Are you searching up, Josh?" and another describing a mix as "apple, banana, chocolate," which is dismissed. The video opens on a wet London sidewalk where the Man in black hoodie (first) gestures near a black telephone booth, while the Man in black hoodie (second) is on his phone. The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie walks past as the group discusses 'edamame,' 'dates,' and plans a trip to 'Tesco,' debating 'ice cream,' 'jelly,' or 'jam.' They walk past a NatWest bank and a red telephone box, entering a restaurant where the group, including the Man in white hoodie, eats edamame from blue bowls and drinks Tsingtao beer. The Man in black hoodie (second) appears tired, prompting jokes about him being asleep. The Man in white hoodie toasts. The scene shifts to a brightly lit Tesco supermarket, where the group, identifying as the 'Sidemen,' browses the ready meals aisle, discussing their wealth. The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie and the Man in black hoodie (first), masked, examine food, while the Man in black hoodie (second), also masked, walks nearby. They debate 'ham chunks' and 'honey roast' ham, emphasizing quality and rejecting 'wafer thin ham.' They move through the store, passing 'Clubcard Prices' and 'Ready Meals,' with the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie adjusting his mask. The bright, clinical lighting of Tesco contrasts with the earlier dim restaurant ambiance and the urban outdoor environment.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a close-up of a McDonald’s self-service kiosk screen, displaying a 3.00 GBP payment prompt, as a voice remarks on the generosity of someone paying for it, followed by laughter. The scene then shifts to April 26th, at a bus stop where four men are seated on a red bench, each holding a pink McDonald’s snack box. The man in the light blue hoodie, wearing grey shorts and orange sneakers, opens his box and eats, while a friend (Man in white hoodie) expresses pessimism, saying, "I don't think we're doing well, guys." Another (Man in black hoodie with green circular logo) confidently counters, "I think we're smashing it, bro," with the man in the light blue hoodie agreeing. Amidst laughter, they discuss being "on E" in a word game. The man in the black hoodie with 'SOMN' on his chest complains, "this is the deadest donut I've ever eaten in my life," though the man in the light blue hoodie finds his "all right." The group continues their word game, trying to guess words for 'E' like "Egg" and "Eggplant." The date changes to June 26th as the scene cuts to a wet city sidewalk where three men, including the man in the black hoodie with 'SOMN' and the man in the teal tie-dye hoodie, are eating McDonald’s fries and burgers. The man in the black hoodie with 'SOMN' takes a large bite of fries, exclaiming, "These are small fries. What the hell? Are we in America?" The scene returns to the bus stop, where the four men are now holding phones and conversing, with the man in the light blue hoodie gesturing as he speaks. Laughter and indistinct chatter continue, punctuated by an exaggerated "Oh my god" from the man in the black hoodie with 'SOMN'. Upbeat electronic music begins to play as they continue their conversation, with one friend (Man in black hoodie with green circular logo) mentioning a "pizza place," prompting a surprised "What?" from the man in the light blue hoodie, as a red double-decker bus passes in the background. A red sign behind them reads 'YOU MUST WEAR A FACE COVERING ON PUBLIC TRANSPORT,' and graffiti is visible on the wall, with wet pavement reflecting the urban surroundings.

The segment opens at a bus stop where four men – one in a light blue hoodie, one in white, one in a black hoodie with a green circular logo, and another in a black hoodie with a beanie – are seated on a red bench under a shelter, eating McDonald’s snacks from pink boxes. Amidst light conversation, a man's voice suggests they're heading for "edamame," with another confirming it's "0.2 miles" away. The Man in light blue hoodie gestures animatedly as a man exclaims, "That was f***ing good," likely referring to their meal, while playful humming and singing punctuate the scene. A man then asks if someone will "go and get a tub of grapes for us," to which another agrees, followed by a playful directive, "Microphone management, go. Send him. 3, 2, 1, race!" The scene then cuts to two other men, one in a teal tie-dye hoodie and another in a black hoodie with 'SOMN' on the chest, walking on a wet city sidewalk, eating McDonald’s fries and burgers. The sounds of indistinct chatter and a "whoosh" are heard as a red double-decker bus and black cabs pass by a Sainsbury’s store. They later regroup outside a modern glass-fronted building, where the first two men join them. The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie hands a bottle to the Man in black hoodie with 'SOMN', who is holding strawberries. The group then transitions to a dimly lit restaurant, where they are seated around a long wooden table, laughing and eating sushi. The lively atmosphere is filled with conversation about food, with a man pointing out "dumplings" and discussing "berry, fruit salad, but look, grape, grape." Another man declares, "I'm very full," but still wants "a grape, obviously." The segment culminates with an excited announcement, "The edamame has arrived!" followed by laughter and a man exclaiming, "Hey boys, no... wait," as they clearly enjoy their social meal together with bottles of Singha beer and other drinks on the table.

The video opens inside a modern, dimly lit 'itsu' restaurant, where a group of five casually dressed men are seated around a long wooden table cluttered with Tsingtao beer, edamame, and chopsticks. They are engaged in lively conversation, punctuated by laughter, as one voice comments on something being "quite nice" and another speculates "where they on right now?" The Man in white hoodie raises his beer glass in a toast, while the Man in black hoodie (first) with 'SOMN' on his chest talks animatedly, and the Man in black hoodie (second) with the green circular logo occasionally films. Their discussion includes a voice suggesting "I think they're finished," met with disagreement, "Finished? No. Even dragging them along," and another remarking, "I don't think they've had a beer," followed by repeated exclamations of "chess." The scene briefly cuts to the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie walking on an urban sidewalk, eating McDonald’s fries and holding a burger, as a voice exclaims "Ham!" and then comments, "Can't get ham in there though." Returning to the restaurant, laughter is heard as a voice points out, "Can we pick up on the fact that how Harry holds a glass?" prompting a challenge, "How would you pick up this?" followed by exclamations of disbelief and laughter, "Look at that! That's how he picks up a glass! What is going on?" The conversation continues with a voice asking, "Boys, I've ever seen you drop a glass like this?" and another instructing, "Use your thumb. Use your thumb," before someone states, "It just doesn't feel natural at all," amidst more laughter. The camera then shifts to an overcast, wet urban sidewalk, showing the Man in black hoodie (first) and the Man in black hoodie (second) standing near a black 'TELEPHONE' booth with a 'WiFi here' sign, a red double-decker bus passing in the background. The Man in black hoodie (first) gestures while speaking, and the Man in black hoodie (second) holds a phone. Their conversation shifts to food, with a voice asking, "Are you searching up, Josh?" and another describing a mix as "apple, banana, chocolate," which is then dismissed as "not good mixes."

The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie walks past, holding a phone, as the group is heard discussing food items like 'edamame' and 'dates', then planning a trip to 'Tesco' which is '240 yards' away, debating whether to get 'ice cream', 'jelly', or 'jam'. The scene transitions to a restaurant interior, where the group, including the Man in white hoodie, is seated at a wooden table, eating edamame from small blue bowls and drinking Tsingtao beer. The Man in black hoodie (second) is seen resting his head in his hand, appearing tired or contemplative, prompting laughter and comments from the group like 'He's gone, he's asleep' and joking about being 'five letters in' to something. The Man in white hoodie raises a glass in a toast. The setting shifts to a brightly lit Tesco supermarket, where the group, now explicitly identifying themselves as the 'Sidemen' and emphasizing their wealth, browses the ready meals aisle. The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie examines packaged food items, while the Man in black hoodie (first) is also seen in the aisle, wearing a face mask and holding a phone, and the Man in black hoodie (second) walks nearby, also masked. They continue their discussion about food, specifically 'ham chunks' and 'honey roast' ham, debating quantities and quality, with one member insisting on 'Tesco's finest' and rejecting 'wafer thin ham' because 'if we're eating, we're eating good'. The group moves through the store, passing shelves labeled 'Clubcard Prices' and 'Ready Meals', with the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie adjusting his mask and looking at products. The lighting is bright and clinical, contrasting with the warm, dim ambiance of the restaurant, capturing a dynamic shift from an urban outdoor environment to a social dining setting and then to a routine, yet lively, grocery shopping experience.

event_blocks.summary: Inside a Tesco supermarket, the Man in black hoodie (first) and the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, both masked, engaged in a conversation about payment for "ham" and "chips" at the checkout, with the Man in black hoodie (first) making a celebratory gesture. The scene shifted to a dimly lit restaurant where the Man in white hoodie and the Man in black hoodie (second) were seated with Tsingtao beer and edamame, discussing plans to get "Fries" and noting "Tessy's around the corner." Outside the Tesco Metro Covent Garden on a wet sidewalk, the three men, now unmasked, ate and drank, discussing the "grim" and "boujee" ham, with the Man in black hoodie (first) pointing animatedly. Later, the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie walked towards a restaurant, and inside, the Man in black hoodie (second) woke up in a restaurant with Tsingtao beer and edamame, exclaiming "Oh my god!" and realizing he had an hour left. Exiting, they passed "Joe & The Juice," "Waterstones Deliveries," and "Slug & Lettuce," discussing "Amorino, ice cream yard" before entering the Amorino café for "chocolate and chips" and "One scoop mango ice cream." They continued past "FAST TRACK" and "COFFEE CULTURE" signs, stopping near a black telephone booth amidst shouts of "KFC!" and "Fries!" A group of five men, including the Man in black hoodie (first), Man in black hoodie (second), Man in light blue hoodie, Man in white hoodie, and Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, walked past a "CONCERT HALL APPROACH SE1" sign, chanting "Energy!" and confirming the location of the nearest KFC. Inside the Amorino café, the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie and Man in black hoodie (second) discussed food preferences, with playful banter about cameras and a strong dislike for "quiche." Exiting, they walked under a blue metal bridge structure. The Man in black hoodie (first) and Man in teal tie-dye hoodie interacted outside a café, requesting "a bottle of water," before the group entered a KFC restaurant. At a self-service kiosk, a "KFC FOUNDATION" item was selected, and the group audibly discussed adding "four" "fries." They then sat at an outdoor table eating small food items. At Amorino, the Man in black hoodie (with tattoos) ate gelato, exclaiming "Oh, I've got brain freeze!" while his companion laughed. At KFC, three men ate fries, discussing a riddle and an alphabet game. The Man in white hoodie, Man in light blue hoodie, and Man in black hoodie (second) ate KFC fries outside the restaurant, discussing "Wiffy," "financial advisor," and "Harry and JJ." At Amorino, the Man in black hoodie (first) and Man in teal tie-dye hoodie enjoyed gelato. The first group of three men walked along a wet urban sidewalk, eating fries, discussing being "full," and brainstorming food items like "grapes," "hamburger," "ice," "jelly," and "kale" for their next stop.

The scene then cuts to the exterior of the Tesco Metro Covent Garden store on a wet city sidewalk, where the three men are now outside. event_blocks.summary: On a wet London sidewalk, the Man in black hoodie (first) animatedly discusses 'dosing to niche,' 'Meech,' and 'Josh ain't got bars' with the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, who interjects with 'Oh yeah' and 'Respect' while eating gelato, before they playfully exchange 'Fuck you's' amidst background music and chatter. As they walk past a blue 'Waterloo Centre' sign and a red Lime JUMP bike, the Man in light blue hoodie and the Man in white hoodie, both masked and the latter holding a KFC bag, pass a 'HOLLAND & BARRETT' store and a 'Thomas Exchange Global Ltd' sign. The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie inquires about grapes, and the Man in black hoodie (first) replies 'Possibly,' remarking 'K will be ashamed of you, Harry.' They enter a convenience store, identified as a 'Kingdom of Sweets,' where shelves display snacks like Walkers, Haribo, and Mentos. Inside, the Man in black hoodie (first) holds a phone, while the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie laments the lack of grapes, confirming the first man's observation. Their conversation touches on 'prattle,' 'five guys hamburger,' and 'Jenny babies,' with the Man in black tracksuit with beanie filming them. Outside a pharmacy, the Man in black hoodie (first) messages 'Freezy' about a 'jam product,' stating 'Here we go, Freezy's on.' Later, passing a barber shop, a 'Tunnel Mee' Lebanese food kiosk, a 'Brewmaster' pub, and an 'Underground' sign, they discuss distance ('400 meters') and shopping ('Sainsbury's, grapes, McDonald's hamburger'). Inside another convenience store, the Man in black hoodie (first) picks up a yellow snack pack, and the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie holds a small container, leading to a debate about eating jam without a spoon and discovering a fruit bag with exactly four grapes. Exiting, they gather outside a McDonald’s, where the Man in white hoodie holds a KFC bag, the Man in black hoodie (second) holds a brown McDonald’s bag, and they discuss their purchases, including grapes, under an overcast sky with wet pavement reflecting city lights.

event_blocks.summary: Outside a McDonald's, four men, including the Man in black hoodie (first) with a silver chain, the Man in light blue hoodie, the Man in white hoodie with a blue mask, and the Man in black hoodie (second) with a green logo, engaged in conversation while eating. Their discussion touched on the burgers' sugar content, with one noting "per 100 grams, it's 66 grams of sugar," and another calculating, "we'd have like 80 grams of sugar each." Later, the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, wearing a 'SOMN' patch, was seen inside a convenience store examining a jar of jam, complaining, "My friends are making me eat fucking jam... a whole tub of jam." Returning to the McDonald's scene, the group continued eating and laughing, discussing the burgers' ingredients, including ketchup, and relief that "it's literally just the meat." The Man in black hoodie (first) appeared surprised, mask-less. event_blocks.summary: The narrative follows a group of men through various London locations, beginning with discussions about finding kale and being "in a jam" as they walk past a steakhouse. They transition to an indoor restaurant setting, enjoying drinks and snacks, including pink ice cream, with exclamations of "Oh, ice cream, come on!" and "Ice cream as well, just in case, you know." The Man in light blue hoodie pours a clear liquid, leading to a collective "Cheers, lads," and playful remarks like "You eating good today, bro," and "We lost." The scene shifts to a grocery store where the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, now masked, browses produce, discussing where to find kale in London and asking "Is there anything smaller?" before commenting, "Just grab a bunch for a month." Later, the group is seen outdoors near a black telephone booth, with the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie holding kale and laughing with others, including the Man in black tracksuit with beanie filming them. Playful banter ensues with comments like "Look at that, some kind of freak," and "Bro, we are. We are freaks. Just walking around with a tree." The Man in black hoodie (second) takes a bite of kale, leading to a reflection, "You know, at the start of this video it was like a good laugh, and now it's just sad." They continue to a Nespresso storefront, with the Man in black hoodie (second) and the Man in black tracksuit with beanie walking and laughing, the former still holding kale. The interaction continues with questions like "Who wants a tree?" and playful demands, "Hey, let go of my kale," and comments, "It tastes of nothing, man." The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, wearing a 'SOMN' patch, is seen playfully eating kale on a city sidewalk, joined by the Man in black hoodie (first) and the Man in black hoodie (second), who also eat kale, amidst remarks about it being "grim" and questions about washing it due to the pandemic. The conversation shifts to walking down a bustling London street, passing a Nespresso store and Whole Foods Market, with the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie still holding his kale. They pass a Pizza Hut, Halifax bank, and a red double-decker bus with British flags. The Man in light blue hoodie and Man in white hoodie join, walking past a bus stop with a "YOU MUST WEAR A FACE COVERING" sign. The group strolls along a sidewalk, passing a red double-decker bus and storefronts, with the Man in black tracksuit with beanie filming. Their banter includes discussions of letters like 'K,' 'JKL,' and food items, shifting to "cannabis lolly" and "lollipop," with excited shouts of "L for lollipop!" They enter the "Kingdom of Sweets," where the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie and Man in black hoodie (first) pick up lollipops. Exiting, upbeat pop music plays, and someone exclaims, "This is a whole vibe, bro!" They continue past an Angus Steakhouse, with the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie holding a blue lollipop and the Man in black hoodie (second) holding a red one. Remarks about "blue tongue" and "color changing" follow. The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie and Man in black hoodie (first) stand near the Angus Steakhouse, laughing with lollipops, discussing lollipop sizes and dust. The Man in black hoodie (first) takes a bite, commenting, "Oh, that's how it looks." As they walk past red telephone boxes and a red double-decker bus, the conversation turns to jelly, with the Man in teal tie-dye hoodie stating, "I don't like jelly either, mate." They enter a Sainsbury’s store, where the Man in white hoodie picks up a pink-lidded Hartley’s jelly cup, exclaiming, "Wibbly wobbly jelly!" and discussing flavors, with the Man in black hoodie (first) shouting, "Yeah, strawberry gang!" Inside, the Man in black hoodie (second) and Man in black hoodie (first) wear masks, while the Man in white hoodie holds the jelly cup amidst laughter and a "Strawberry gang!" chant. Exiting, they pass a currency exchange shop and a "LOOK LEFT" sign. The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie asks, "What's after L?" to which the Man in black hoodie (first) jokingly replies, "Uh, we don't, we don't take L's, so can't be us." They engage in a word association game, listing "M, N, O, V," "Mango," and "Meningitis." The scene opens with a man in a light blue and white hoodie exiting a Co-op store, adjusting his face mask, joined by a man in a white hoodie eating from a small cup, discussing "jelly" and "munch." The scene shifts to a group near an HSBC UK bank with a red double-decker bus passing. The man in the white hoodie blows a large red bubble with gum, which pops loudly, eliciting laughter. The group moves to a convenience store, where the man in the black hoodie with the green logo holds a pink-lidded cup, and the man in the white hoodie eats from a similar cup. The scene transitions to a busy street where a man in a teal tie-dye hoodie with a 'SOMN' patch holds a blue lollipop, laughing, joined by a man in a black hoodie with 'SOMN' on the chest, holding Haribo Chamallows and eating a lollipop. A debate erupts about "chamallows" versus "marshmallows," leading to playful insults. The scene opens on a wet, overcast London street with wet pavement, featuring storefronts like 'MORMON' and 'Shake Shack'. The Man in black hoodie (first), with visible hand tattoos and wearing a 'SOMN' hoodie, is animated, eating a pink lollipop, exclaiming "Simon Sunday!" and declaring "all we've done is eat sugar, lads." The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, holding a blue lollipop and Haribo Chamallows, expresses his desire for "nuts" from "West End foods." The Man in black hoodie (second), in a black beanie and a hoodie with a green logo, smiles and gestures. The group walks along the sidewalk, passing a 'SOUVENIRS' kiosk, discussing TikTok dances and changing their 'team to cringe'. The scene opens on a wet, overcast London sidewalk near Leicester Square, where the Man in black hoodie (first), Man in teal tie-dye hoodie, and Man in black hoodie (second) stand before a 'Cork & Bottle' vape shop. The Man in black hoodie (first) hands a small object to the Man in black hoodie (second), prompting laughter and playful banter, with voices heard asking "What nut do you want?" and a playful complaint, "You nutted in my hand." As the camera pans to reveal urban elements like 'West End Foods' and 'Souvenirs' shops, the group moves along, passing the 'Hippodrome' and 'Casino'. They approach a red double-decker bus and a construction site, where the Man in black hoodie (first) interacts with the Man in white hoodie and Man in light blue hoodie, both blowing red gum bubbles. A voice exclaims, "Oh, what's that?" as an item is handed over, leading to a discussion about an "oat bar" and getting it "approved by Freezy." The Man in black hoodie (first) continues filming with a stabilizer as the Man in light blue hoodie adjusts his black face mask. The group then enters a brightly lit convenience store, browsing snack aisles, while voices discuss "Jay's done," playfully chant "KSI," and suggest going back to "get Kale," before deciding to go to someone's place as it's "a long day." The Man in black hoodie (second) holds Haribo Chamallows and a soda, and the Man in black hoodie (first) picks up a pink-lidded cup. The scene concludes with the group exiting the store, laughing and gesturing, as the Man in black hoodie (first) continues filming.

The video opens on a bustling city sidewalk in front of the Hippodrome Casino, where three men walk and converse amidst the sounds of urban activity. The man in the black 'SOMN' hoodie remarks, "We're on P," and later, "The boys are defeated now. I feel like we're walking in slow motion." The man in the teal tie-dye hoodie, who has a slightly higher-pitched voice, adds, "I want to keep up," and then, "I'm heavy. I'm heavy." The man in the black hoodie with the green circular logo, speaking with a lively tone, humorously observes, "There's about 15 different foods that should not be together and they're inside us right now," eliciting laughter. The scene transitions to the group seated at an outdoor restaurant, enjoying pizza and soda. The man in the black 'SOMN' hoodie, with a deeper voice, questions, "Why do we do eating videos?" and "Why do you guys watch eating videos so much?" The teal hoodie man responds, "I don't know, bro." The green logo man explains, "Every time we're like, at the end of an eating video, we say we're never doing an eating video like that again," to which the 'SOMN' man interjects, "And then it gets 10 million views." The man in the teal hoodie stands, gestures, and laughs, declaring, "If this video don't bang, bro, I'm I'm switching it, you lot. You lot fucking pagans." The green logo man laughs heartily and counters, "No, but if it doesn't bang, we can stop eating for content." The teal hoodie man then states, "I hate eating." The 'SOMN' man, leaning back, shares, "However, like I said, we have intel. And apparently, they're lagging behind," with the green logo man adding, "I wouldn't be surprised." The 'SOMN' man continues, "So we're taking this time now to sit and just digest." The green logo man advises, "Look, it's a marathon, not a sprint," as the 'SOMN' man plans, "We're planning our next..." The teal hoodie man sips from his water bottle, and the 'SOMN' man takes a large bite of pizza, lamenting, "I'm not going to be able to do it off this." The teal hoodie man suggests, "Quinoa," which the green logo man approves, "Quinoa's good." The 'SOMN' man groans, "Another carbohydrate, for fuck's sake!" The green logo man concludes, "We've had a lot of everything, man," while the 'SOMN' man observes, "It looks like decent pizza." Throughout, the lively atmosphere is punctuated by laughter and the sounds of the city and restaurant, with the on-screen text '15/26' visible.

raw_segments: The video opens in an overcast Trafalgar Square, London, with the iconic National Gallery and Nelson’s Column in the background, as a group of five casually dressed men gather around a large fountain. The atmosphere is immediately lively with excited chatter and laughter, as someone repeatedly asks, "is this fun?" The Man in teal tie-dye hoodie is seated on the fountain’s edge, laughing heartily and gesturing animatedly while speaking, his energy contributing to the group's jovial mood. Beside him, the Man in black hoodie (first) and the Man in black hoodie (second), who holds a phone, are also seated, engaged in the continuous, joking conversation. The Man in light blue hoodie and the Man in white hoodie stand nearby, smiling and laughing, occasionally gesturing toward the seated trio. The group discusses past events, with one person recalling, "We got to K," and another asking, "Did you do K or not?" followed by laughter and a declaration, "that's my brain's gone, bro." They recount a story about trying to get "quesadillas" but finding the place "shut," leading to exclamations like "I'm fucked!" and deciding to "sack it off" and sit at the fountain. Celebratory shouts of "Oh, so we won!" and "Congratulations!" erupt, punctuated by more laughter. The conversation shifts to a humorous anecdote, with someone asking, "Why are we laughing at P? Because he goes, 'pineapple!'" prompting further amusement. The group continues to share stories, noting, "We worked out we averaged the food every 14 minutes" and "we were just eating on the go." A whimsical digital overlay of a giant ice cream sundae with a cherry on top floats above the fountain, adding a playful, surreal element to the scene. As a red double-decker bus passes and pigeons scatter across the plaza, the camera dynamically captures the group's expressions and interactions, highlighting their evident camaraderie and lighthearted social gathering in the grand historic setting. They conclude their discussion with a playful debate, "You may think that we won, but I think we actually lost," followed by confident assertions of "I feel great!" and more laughter, underscoring their relaxed and fun-filled interaction.

The Man in black tracksuit with beanie filmed the group, some seated on the fountain's edge, others standing, as remarks like "We're gonna have to hit it until the video ended" were made, concluding with "Right, well, nice one, guys. Thanks for watching, everyone. Sidemen to ten mil!" and an enthusiastic "Whoop!"
